article: City officials in Austin said a new pilot program will use artificial intelligence to route residents to the right public services more quickly. The city manager said the tool will answer routine questions, flag urgent requests, and forward complex cases to staff. Privacy officers reviewed the vendor contract before launch, and the pilot will be audited after ninety days.
summary: Austin launched an AI service pilot to handle routine resident requests while staff review privacy and audit results.

article: European negotiators reached a preliminary climate agreement after two days of talks in Brussels. The plan would expand funding for flood defenses, require faster reporting from heavy emitters, and create a joint research pool for battery storage. Several ministers said the final vote could happen next month if legal language is completed on schedule.
summary: European officials agreed on a draft climate plan focused on flood funding, emissions reporting, and battery research.

article: The Riverside Tigers announced a stadium renovation that will add shaded seating, wider concourses, and upgraded training rooms before next season. Team executives said local contractors will handle most of the work, while city leaders expect nearby restaurants to benefit from extra foot traffic during construction and after reopening.
summary: The Riverside Tigers approved a stadium upgrade that expands seating comfort, improves facilities, and is expected to help nearby businesses.

article: The central bank kept interest rates unchanged on Thursday, saying inflation has cooled but remains above its long term target. Policymakers noted that wage growth is still strong and consumer spending has held up better than expected. Analysts said the statement leaves room for one additional cut later this year if energy prices remain stable.
summary: The central bank held rates steady and signaled that a later cut is possible if inflation continues to ease.

article: A suburban school district introduced a tutoring program for middle school students who fell behind in math during last year's weather closures. Teachers will offer after school sessions three times a week, and buses will run later on tutoring days. Administrators said the district used state recovery funds to keep the program free for families.
summary: The district started free after school math tutoring with transportation support for students affected by prior closures.

article: Doctors at North Valley Hospital began testing an AI assisted note taking system to reduce time spent on documentation during patient visits. Hospital leaders said the software drafts visit summaries for review but does not replace clinical judgment. Nurses and physicians will report weekly on accuracy, workload, and patient communication before any wider rollout.
summary: North Valley Hospital is testing AI generated visit notes to reduce paperwork while clinicians monitor accuracy and patient communication.

article: Rail workers in the capital suspended a planned strike after union leaders reached a temporary pay deal with transit authorities late Friday. The agreement includes a one time bonus, a smaller schedule change, and a commitment to resume negotiations in April. Commuters were told normal weekend service would continue while both sides review the written proposal.
summary: A temporary pay deal paused the rail strike, allowing normal weekend service to continue while talks resume in April.

article: A private aerospace company completed its third successful satellite launch of the quarter, putting twelve weather monitoring payloads into low Earth orbit. Engineers said the mission used a lighter fuel tank and a revised guidance sequence that shortened launch preparation by several hours. Customers from Brazil, Kenya, and Japan said the faster turnaround could lower future costs.
summary: The company placed twelve weather satellites into orbit and said new engineering changes could reduce future launch costs.
